items.title: 'Can't put a figure on it': Greyhound industry rejects $14m exit offer

children.content: As a final decision looms for Tasmania's greyhound industry on whether it will be phased out, breeders, trainers and other industry participants reject an exit offer from the state government.

avatar.alt: A man with light green glasses smiles.

items.title: Locals salty over being 'forced to listen to music that is not our taste'

children.content: With yet another venue facing complaints about noise from residents, performers say Tasmania runs the risk of becoming a dead zone for live music.

image.altText: Unidentified male guitarist plays instrument.

items.title: Student vaccinated without consent prompts council concern

children.content: A Tasmanian mayor is calling on the state government to take on responsibility for running school immunisation programs, after a student was vaccinated without caregiver consent.

image.altText: A student receives a measles vaccine injection at the Ecole Polytechnique Federale de Lausanne (EPFL).

items.title: How did a 3km hike go so wrong? Retracing Celine Cremer's final steps

children.content: Belgian backpacker Celine Cremer was on the trip of a lifetime, but on a hike in Tasmania she left the trail and was never seen again. It took a private investigator, a YouTuber, loved ones and an army of volunteers to find answers.

items.title: Loved, but costly — is the end near for this heritage railway?

children.content: The West Coast Wilderness Railway in Tasmania is a beloved attraction and an important employer. The state government says it wants the railway to have a sustainable future, but hasn't said how that could be achieved.

image.altText: Locomotive travels through a forest railway track.

items.title: Queen Mary of Denmark's father dies in Tasmania aged 84

children.content: The Australian-born Queen of Denmark last visited her father Professor John Donaldson during a royal visit to Tasmania last month.

image.altText: A black and white photo of an older bearded man facing away from the camera looking pensive.

items.title: Terminaly ill MP says ban on telehealth for assisted dying must change

children.content: A Labor politician living with MND has written to the attorney-general calling for laws banning doctors from discussing voluntary assisted dying during telehealth appointments to be revised.

items.title: Ex-One Nation candidate wants Hanson 'held accountable' for rehiring rapist ex-staffer

children.content: Two former One Nation candidates have accused party leader Pauline Hanson and chief of staff James Ashby of dismissing concerns raised internally about the convicted rapist and former campaign director Sean Black.

items.title: Social media checks for all visa applicants under Angus Taylor plan

children.content: Social media screening would be introduced for all visa applicants under an immigration policy to be revealed by Opposition leader Angus Taylor.

items.title: Trump's defamation lawsuit against Wall Street Journal over Epstein story dismissed

children.content: The case related to a Wall Street Journal article that described a sexually suggestive letter that the newspaper said was included in a 2003 album compiled for Epstein’s 50th birthday.

image.altText: Rupert Murdoch and Donald Trump on a golf course.

items.title: Hungarian voters showed little regard for Donald Trump's wishes

children.content: A record number of Hungarians turned out not just to send a strong message to their far-right prime minister, but to reject Donald Trump.

image.altText: Viktor Orban concedes the hungarian election on stage surrounded by supporters

items.title: As the Sussexes return to Australia, 'unusual' commercial engagements are raising eyebrows

children.content: Harry and Meghan will be travelling under their titles of Duke and Duchess of Sussex, and will appear at two major, ticketed events as well as making the rounds of various charities and causes.

items.title: Feral cat numbers spark bounty push across outback Queensland

children.content: Regional councils are paying for feral cats as populations boom in outback Queensland, but experts are not convinced the strategy will help.

items.title: The neuroscience behind synaesthesia paints a surprising inner world

children.content: Have you ever tasted a word, or seen colours while listening to music? If you have, you may be among the 1 to 4 per cent of people who have a fascinating trait known as synaesthesia.

image.altText: Woman in headphones looks at bright spectrum of lights

items.title: How these queer couples split the mental load might surprise you

children.content: After publishing our story on the four stages of the mental load, we asked to hear from queer couples about how they divide and conquer the cognitive and emotional labour at home.

children.content: Murri Watch says the service closure will pose a significant risk to the safety and wellbeing of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander children and young people.

items.title: World-famous Horizontal Falls to allow more tourists, with cultural focus

items.title: Australia's LNG exporters will face a tougher future, new report says

children.content: The long-term outlook for Australia's liquefied natural gas exports is structurally constrained by declining demand, and the current war in the Middle East may hasten that shift away from gas, a new report claims.

image.altText: An LNG flare flames out from a stack.

items.title: Booking.com customers involved in possible data and security breach

children.content: Some Booking.com customers are being warned their personal information may have been accessed by unauthorised third parties, prompting fresh concerns about the security of travellers' data.

items.title: Albanese names new defence force chief

children.content: Vice Admiral Mark Hammond, who has spent 40 years in the navy, will replace current defence force chief David Johnston when he retires in July.

items.title: Reptile remains believed to be a crocodile found dumped on side of road

children.content: Reptile remains — which are believed to be from a crocodile — have been collected by authorities after they were found dumped on the side of an Adelaide road.

items.title: Massive hospital spend creates more questions than answers

children.content: The WA government is splashing an additional $1.5 billion on health, bringing the state's total hospital spend to $5.5 billion over the next four years. But where exactly will the extra money go?

items.title: Australia's best inventor you've never heard of

children.content: Solar power is forecast to overtake coal as the world's biggest source of electricity next year, and its growth shows no signs of slowing down. Behind its success is a little-known Australian inventor.

items.title: Growers could 'pull the pin' as fuel costs soar

children.content: As fuel prices remain high amid the war in the Middle East, growers and fishers are among those having to make tough decisions to remain viable.

image.altText: Vicki holds a fish with tongs and smiles.

items.title: Backyard astronomer who helped to discover 100 distant planets awarded medal

children.content: From a backyard observatory in regional Victoria, Gippsland astronomer Chris Stockdale spends his nights helping confirm distant planets by tracking tiny changes in starlight that reveal new worlds beyond our solar system.

image.altText: A man wearing glasses and a blue button-down shirt, holding a box and grabbing a large telescope

items.title: Canada has flipped the script on child care. Could Australia copy?

children.content: Australia definitely won't nationalise child care, even though the data suggests there's a strong case. Perhaps a more feasible idea would be to copy what Canada has done.
